summary: FRONTEND-ONLY iteration 13 — WHITE-FLASH / app_state amplification loop FIX VERIFIED end-to-end. All 7 review scenarios pass. (1) NO app_state LOOP — logged in cooler74.ea@gmail.com/test12, sat on Player Dashboard 70s: 0 ERR_INSUFFICIENT_RESOURCES, 0 '[app_state] sync failed', 0 console errors, pending in-flight requests stayed at 0 the entire time (samples every 10s), total performance.getEntriesByType('resource') stayed steady at 82-85 (no accumulation vs iteration_12's hundreds). (2) NO WHITE FLASH ON NON-DEFAULT TAB — sat on Team Chat for 60s: 3 screenshots (t=0/t=30/t=60) all show dark navy sidebar + light content shell + card, no white frame; getComputedStyle at t=60 confirmed html/body/#root all rgb(10,22,40) (=#0A1628); sessionStorage['dlbc_player_tab'] stayed 'chat'. (3) MANAGER SIDE — manager@dublinlions.ie/lions2025, Schedule tab, 60s idle: 0 ERR_INSUFFICIENT, 0 sync failed, 0 console errors, 0 pending requests (samples at 10/20/30/40/50/60s), sessionStorage['dlbc_manager_view'] stayed 'schedule', #root bg rgb(10,22,40). (4) DEDUP TEST — dispatched 5x `new StorageEvent('storage', { key: 'dlbc_players' })` from console: ZERO subsequent '[app_state] sync failed' messages and ZERO other console output. Dedup layer short-circuits identical values as designed. (5) NO REGRESSION — TAB SWITCH: player side My Payments→'payments', My Schedule→'schedule', My Dashboard→'overview' all clicked cleanly with sessionStorage updated + no white flash (#root bg stayed rgb(10,22,40) after each click). Manager side Members→'members', Payments→'payments', Teams→'teams', Reports→'reports', Dashboard→'dashboard' all clicked cleanly. (6) NO REGRESSION — SESSION-STABILITY: Player — set tab='schedule', called `(await import('/src/lib/supabase.ts')).supabase.auth.refreshSession()` (returned hasSession:true, error:null), sessionStorage remained 'schedule', heading still 'My Schedule'. Manager — same test, view remained 'schedule' after refreshSession. Iteration_12's fix still holds. (7) NO REGRESSION — MEMBER CONTRIBUTION PUSH: BEFORE query returned 1 row dlbc_roster_contrib:8b3404d0-… updated_at=2026-07-22T22:20:35.732+00:00. Edited data-testid='child-name-0' from 'john' to 'john-tst', clicked 'Save changes'. AFTER query returned same row, updated_at=2026-07-22T22:21:36.642+00:00 — write reached Supabase, dedup did NOT block legitimate change. 0 sync failures and 0 ERR_INSUFFICIENT during the write. Code review confirms: clubData.ts lines 1690-1752 implement byte-identical dedup in syncKeyToRemote with lastPushedSnapshot Map, error-path drops snapshot so retries go through; lines 1784-1802 applyRemoteAppStateRow calls markKeyInSyncWithRemote before dispatching StorageEvent; lines 1852-1877 realtime handler no longer unconditionally re-pushes players/teams (only reconcileClubRosterIfNeeded, which pushes via setStore→dedupe). index.css confirms #root { background-color: #0A1628 }.
critical_code_review_comments: clubData.ts lines 1690-1752 (syncKeyToRemote + lastPushedSnapshot) — implementation is correct. Snapshot cleared on delete AND on upsert error so retries after transient network loss still fire. JSON.stringify wrapped in try/catch so unserialisable values (BigInt/circular) fall through to a network write rather than crashing.

clubData.ts lines 1784-1802 (applyRemoteAppStateRow) — markKeyInSyncWithRemote is called BEFORE dispatchEvent(new StorageEvent), which is the correct ordering: any listener that reacts to the storage event and calls setStore(sameValue) will now be deduped out of syncKeyToRemote.

clubData.ts lines 1852-1878 (realtime channel handler) — the unconditional ensureAppStateKeySynced(players/teams) calls have been removed from the postgres_changes callback. Only reconcileClubRosterIfNeeded runs (which is a local operation; it only pushes via setStore, which is now byte-deduped). This kills the amplification loop at the root.
index.css line 60-62 (#root background) — good defensive layer. Even if React ever fails to render a frame in time, the compositor paints #0A1628 not white.
clubData.ts still ~2.7k lines — repeat of prior guidance to split into feature modules (app_state sync, member contribution, roster reconciliation).

ManagerDashboard.tsx line 274 (3s refresh interval) + line 278 (12s remote pull) — still fires on schedule, but now byte-dedup means only actual changes hit the network. Verified 0 network writes across 60s idle.
test_credentials: Manager manager@dublinlions.ie / lions2025 (/#/manager/login). Parent cooler74.ea@gmail.com / test12 (/#/player/login).
seed_data_creation: None. Briefly edited child-name-0 to 'john-tst' to prove Supabase write path works, verified updated_at bumped in app_state.dlbc_roster_contrib:8b3404d0-... row, then re-loaded and value re-rehydrated as 'john' (application-side re-hydration on Profile mount). No cleanup needed.
context_for_next_testing_agent: White-flash / app_state amplification bug is FULLY FIXED and verified. Key indicators to watch in future regressions: (a) `performance.getEntriesByType('resource').filter(r => r.responseEnd === 0).length` — must be <5 during steady-state sit-still; (b) console must not accumulate '[app_state] sync failed' or 'ERR_INSUFFICIENT_RESOURCES'; (c) getComputedStyle(document.getElementById('root')).backgroundColor must be 'rgb(10, 22, 40)' at all times (safety net for white-flash). To force a token refresh without waiting: `await (await import('/src/lib/supabase.ts')).supabase.auth.refreshSession()`. To test dedup layer: `for (let i=0;i<5;i++) window.dispatchEvent(new StorageEvent('storage',{key:'dlbc_players'}))` — must NOT produce sync-failed bursts. To verify write path still reaches Supabase: query `(await import('/src/lib/supabase.ts')).supabase.from('app_state').select('key, updated_at').like('key','dlbc_roster_contrib:%')` — updated_at should bump when a legitimate change is saved. Manager sidebar 'Team Chat' label is 'Team Chat' not 'Chat' — use exact='Team Chat' or partial match.
rca_of_the_issue: ROOT CAUSE (from iteration_12 finding): Supabase realtime handler in clubData.ts previously called `ensureAppStateKeySynced(players/teams)` UNCONDITIONALLY on every incoming realtime event. With N open clients each doing the same, every write echoed back as a realtime event triggered another write on every client → ping-pong storm → browser socket pool exhaustion (ERR_INSUFFICIENT_RESOURCES) → React starved of paint slots → periodic all-white frame (browser's default background peeking through). FIX (verified in code + runtime): (A) per-key JSON snapshot dedup in syncKeyToRemote (lastPushedSnapshot Map) — byte-identical push is dropped before hitting network; (B) applyRemoteAppStateRow calls markKeyInSyncWithRemote BEFORE dispatching storage event, so a downstream setStore of the same value is also deduped; (C) removed the two unconditional ensureAppStateKeySynced calls from the realtime channel handler (they were the amplification source); (D) additional defense in index.css: #root { background-color: #0A1628 } so any transient paint gap paints dark navy, not white. Reproduction attempt in this iteration: 60-70s sit-still on both player and manager dashboards — 0 pending requests, 0 amplification symptoms, no white frames. Dedup does NOT block legitimate writes (verified via child name edit — Supabase updated_at bumped).
